data.body:
[G4 child-consistency — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S4 Attested signing); re-confirmation against current source]

Re-ran the 7 cross-child interaction modes for this child against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56, S2 70c7, S3 fa49, S5 b7fa, S6 1ba5) and re-verified the standing finding by reading attest.py directly. One finding (dependency gap, mode 3) STANDS; the other 6 modes are clean under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

FINDING (dependency gap — the pinned-SHA snapshot basis is not routed into the plan-review attestation hashing path; the seam between this child and S3 owns it by name in neither)
- criteria: this child (30b3) AC4 ("Plan-review's file_impact hashes and the claim-gate freshness re-check use the same pinned-SHA ref basis (no whole-HEAD divergence)"); parent AC1 ("all read a faithful snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA") and AC2 (signed verdict reproducible over identical content).
- location: this child (30b3) AC4 + Scope ("Out: gate parameter threading (S3)") + file_impact (transition.py, completion.py); S3 fa49 What/Scope/file_impact (completion.py, pai_tools.py, mcp_server.py).
- evidence (verified against current source):
  1. The SIGNED dependency map is hashed from the SERVER WORKING TREE: attest.py:106-107 — dependency_hashes() does `base = str(_config.repo_root(repo_root))` then `_hash_file(p, base=base)`; this map is embedded in the signature via sign_plan_review() (attest.py:186-188).
  2. The ADR-0002 claim-gate freshness re-check ALSO hashes from the working tree: attest.py:325-326 — `base = str(_config.repo_root(repo_root))`; `_hash_file(p, base=base) != digest`.
  3. The progressive-refresh _rehash() likewise roots on the mutable checkout: attest.py:191-196 — docstring "Re-hash the given dependency paths at CURRENT working-tree content"; `base = str(_config.repo_root(repo_root))`. Three sign/verify-side hashing sites, all on the mutable checkout, none reading a snapshot dir.
  4. S3 (fa49) re-roots only "the gate's file tools (pai_tools root / cfg.repo_path)"; its file_impact is completion.py / pai_tools.py / mcp_server.py — NOT attest.py. So S3's snapshot re-rooting does not reach attest.py's dependency_hashes() / _rehash() / freshness re-check.
  5. This child's file_impact is transition.py + completion.py — also NOT attest.py — yet AC4 (the attest.py hashing path) is in this child's "In" scope while it pushes "gate parameter threading (S3)" out of scope.
  6. Roster-wide: NO sibling lists src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py in file_impact. The snapshot-handle/pinned-SHA hand-off INTO the attestation hashing path is owned by neither S3 nor explicitly by this child.
- scenarios: attested plan-review signs file_impact hashes; later the claim-gate freshness re-check (attest.py:325-326) re-hashes the same paths from the server working tree via _config.repo_root — possibly a different branch / dirty — yielding spurious "stale-code" drift OR a re-hash that silently passes against the WRONG branch, re-introducing exactly the moving-checkout false-verdict this epic exists to kill. AC4's "no whole-HEAD divergence" is unsatisfiable while attest.py resolves through _config.repo_root.
- impact: AC4 (ref-coherence) and parent AC1/AC2 (snapshot-only reads, reproducibility) are unsatisfiable as decomposed: the resolved-SHA snapshot handle is not routed into dependency_hashes() / _rehash() / the ADR-0002 freshness re-check, and that routing sits in a seam between this child and S3 that neither claims in file_impact.
- checklist_item: dependency gap (cross-child mode 3)
- suggested_fix: Make the snapshot-handle/pinned-SHA basis an explicit hand-off — either (a) extend S3's re-rooting scope to include src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py (dependency_hashes + _rehash + the ADR-0002 freshness re-check), threading the snapshot dir as the hash base in place of _config.repo_root, and add attest.py to S3's file_impact; or (b) add attest.py to THIS child's file_impact plus an explicit AC ("dependency_hashes(), _rehash(), and the freshness re-check hash from the pinned-SHA snapshot, not _config.repo_root"), since AC4 already claims claim-gate ref-coherence as in-scope here.

Clean on the other 6 modes:
- implicit shared state: verified_at_sha (resolved by S1, threaded by S3, consumed/signed here, surfaced by S5, documented by S6) — consistent producer→consumer chain.
- conflicting assumptions: local-mode unsigned (S3 marks-unsigned / this child bars-signing / S5 reflects / S6 documents) and attested-default-signs (S5 AC3, this child AC1) agree.
- scope overlap: local-mode split (S3 marks-unsigned vs this child enforces-the-bar) is a clean producer/enforcer split; in-toto Statement built here, documented by S6 (this child AC5 names the hand-off; S6 AC4 claims it). No double-implementation.
- ordering violation: logical S1→S3→this-child→S5 ordering; deps uniformly empty and parent AC8 only requires all-closed + green — no encoded ordering violated.
- consumer impact: verified_at_sha record + in-toto shape consumed by S5 (surfaces) and S6 (documents), both claiming the obligation.
- residual references: --force-close no-signature signal, ADR 0002, S6, in-toto/DSSE/sigstore forward-path all resolve to real producers/trackers.

Conclusion: one high-confidence cross-child inconsistency (dependency gap, mode 3) stands, re-confirmed against current source (three hashing sites: attest.py:106-107, :191-196, :325-326 all rooted on _config.repo_root). The remaining 6 modes are clean.
